misconfig_patterns: --insecure is canonical in every official quickstart, compose file, and tutorial
Node authentication requires TLS (exit code 303 without certs), so the easy path ships with no node-auth at all
SuperExec HMAC is experimental and off by default
Exec.ListRuns + Exec.StreamLogs leak run IDs, FAB hash, client counts, dataset names and hyperparameters via logs

active_probe.false_positive_check: Require flwr.proto.* service in reflection enum, not just any gRPC handler

deployment_tells: All HTTP/2 gRPC — Shodan-dark on http.html filters; routes to Censys + active full-handshake
Realistic exposure tier: academic / .edu / biomedical research (cross-ref Cat-49 univ map)
Rhino FCP partnership Feb 2025 wraps pharma deployments — those are hardened
pivot_paths: gRPC reflection on 9093 -> enumerate flwr.proto.exec.v1.Exec service descriptor
Exec.StreamLogs -> log-leaked dataset names, hyperparameters (read-only, in-scope)
Exec.StartRun -> integrity write line, DO NOT CROSS
vulnerabilities: Zero CVEs against flwrlabs/flower as of 2026-06-09
CVE-2022-30034 is the unrelated Celery web UI 'Flower' — common conflation
Green-field surface; auth-on-default disclosure-pressure curve (Insight #40) has not landed
